entries.title: Commentary on Proverbs on proverbs 10:3
entries.excerpt: The Lord will not let the soul of the righteous be famished, etc. And if at any time the unjust afflict or even kill the righteous with famine, they will not hurt his soul, whom the Lord will comfort in the future life with the glory of His visitation, who also rightly turns the same traps of the wicked back upon them by judgment.

entries.title: Treatise VIII. On Works and Alms 11 on proverbs 10:3
entries.excerpt: Do you fear that your patrimony may fail if you begin to act generously from it? For when did it happen that resources could fail a just person, when it is written, "The Lord will not afflict the soul of the just with famine."

entries.title: SCHOLIA ON PROVERBS 118:10.3 on proverbs 10:3
entries.excerpt: If the life of the wicked is malicious and the Lord will overturn it, then it is clear that at some point the wicked will no longer be wicked. For, after that "reversal," "the Lord will pass the kingdom over to God the Father," so that God may be "all in all."
